title: Head First Design Patterns
subtitle: A Brain-Friendly Guide
author: Eric Freeman, Elisabeth Robson, Bert Bates, Kathy Sierra
themes: Learning design patterns through visual, engaging, multi-sensory techniques
Object-oriented design principles: encapsulate what varies, favor composition, program to interfaces
The 14+ foundational Gang of Four patterns explained with memorable real-world analogies
Building extensible and maintainable object-oriented software
Patterns as a shared vocabulary for communicating design intent
awards: One of the best-selling programming books of all time
Transformed how design patterns are taught to a generation of developers
excerpt: Head First Design Patterns is the most accessible introduction to software design patterns ever written. Using the latest research in cognitive science, the book delivers the 14+ most important Gang of Four patterns through a visually rich, conversational format with memorable analogies, exercises, and humor. More than a pattern catalog, it teaches the object-oriented design principles that make patterns work.
